input.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

raw.headline: Wales’ route to World Cup 2026 in full after play-off draw

winner: Wales has mapped out its route to the 2026 World Cup — now all they need is a GPS and a willingness to believe that "play-off draw" means something other than a game of Connect Four with traffic cones.

candidates.text: Wales has mapped out its route to the 2026 World Cup — now all they need is a GPS and a willingness to believe that "play-off draw" means something other than a game of Connect Four with traffic cones.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific comparison to Connect Four with traffic cones adds humor; concise and natural language."}

candidates.text: Wales is so excited about reaching the World Cup that they've already started drafting their national anthem—with extra verses for every time someone says "draw.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clever and specific, playing on the word 'draw' in both the play-off context and the national anthem. It's concise and has a nice surprise element, though it could be slightly more original."}

candidates.text: Wales has officially secured their path to the World Cup, so I guess you could say they’re finally on the right track—or at least the right road.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise and uses a play on 'right track' and 'right road,' which ties into the headline's mention of a route. It’s somewhat surprising and specific to Wales’ situation, though the humor is mild and could be more impactful."}

candidates.text: Wales has mapped out their route to the World Cup – and it looks like a long walk through a rugby field with a map that says "This way to 2026, via a play-off draw.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific reference to the rugby field and play-off draw, and includes a mild surprise with the map detail. However, it could be more original and less reliant on a common metaphor (long walk)."}

candidates.text: Wales finally figured out their route to the 2026 World Cup, which is a relief because they were starting to think they were just wandering around the stadium looking for the nearest exit.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a relatable metaphor (wandering around looking for the nearest exit), and ties into the specific context of Wales' World Cup journey. However, it lacks some surprise and could be more specific about the play-off draw."}

candidates.text: The Welsh football team is celebrating their qualification for the World Cup—because they finally found a penalty area large enough for all their goalposts.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat specific and has a mild surprise element, but the humor is weak and relies on a forced pun that doesn't strongly connect to the headline or the context of Wales qualifying for the World Cup."}

candidates.text: Wales qualified for the World Cup, which is great—now they just have to figure out how to lose.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat funny and specific to Wales' situation, but the punchline relies on a negative stereotype (losing) that may not land as well with all audiences."}

candidates.text: Wales has officially completed their route to the World Cup, though they are currently stuck in a dead end because they didn't bother to bring a map or a vehicle.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the 'dead end' metaphor, but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about Wales or the World Cup context. The humor is mild and not particularly surprising."}

candidates.text: Wales' plan for the World Cup is now set — they'll just have to remember how to get back from the playoffs.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with the wordplay on 'get back from the playoffs,' but it's a bit generic and lacks specificity about Wales or the World Cup context."}
